channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Six agricultural workers die as speeding Thar collides with autorickshaw in Chikkaballapur
item.description: On Sunday afternoon, a tragic accident occurred in Chikkaballapur district of Karnataka, resulting in the death of six people and injuring four others. The incident involved a speeding Mahindra Thar t...

item.title: Legal community survey signals growing concern over Trump administration's impact on rule of law
item.description: A recent discussion among legal experts highlights growing alarm within the U.S. legal community regarding President Donald Trump’s second administration and its impact on the rule of law. Attorneys B...

item.title: Reassessing Pakistan’s deterrence doctrine in a changing South Asian battlefield
item.description: The article reflects on how South Asia’s strategic environment has evolved 28 years after Pakistan’s nuclear tests at Chagai, arguing that traditional deterrence assumptions are increasingly under str...

item.title: FG Greenlights Massive Upgrade of Sports Facilities Across Nigeria
item.description: The Federal Government don finally give green light for nationwide sports infrastructure overhaul. Abuja’s Moshood Abiola National Stadium dey front and centre for major renovation to meet internation...

item.title: Kerala UDF government’s proposal on menstrual leave for schoolgirls draws debate over privacy and implementation
item.description: The UDF government’s proposal in Kerala to introduce up to three days of menstrual leave per month for schoolgirls has triggered a public debate, with several political leaders expressing concerns ove...

item.title: Rumours and fan theories circulate about Taylor Swift’s possible involvement in Toy Story 5
item.description: The article discusses ongoing speculation among fans about whether global pop star Taylor Swift could be involved in the upcoming animated film ‘Toy Story 5’. According to the report from ABS-CBN Ente...

item.title: Oil Prices Surge Amid Renewed Israel-Hezbollah Conflict in Lebanon
item.description: On 1st June 2026, oil prices experienced a notable rise following Israel's decision to advance troops further into Lebanon in ongoing clashes with the Iranian-backed Hezbollah militant group. This esc...

item.title: DC Area Weather Update: Clear Skies and Pleasant Conditions Expected
item.description: On May 30, 2026, the Capital Weather Gang provided live updates on weather conditions in the Washington, D.C. area. The forecast indicates clear skies tonight with a bright moon, making for an enjoyab...

item.title: Canadian economic slowdown triggers debate over possible recession amid weak GDP data
item.description: Recent economic data from Statistics Canada showing two consecutive quarters of weak growth has intensified debate in Ottawa and on Bay Street about whether Canada is approaching or already in a reces...

item.title: NIA Deputy Superintendent Dies in Motorcycle Collision on Delhi-Lucknow Highway
item.description: In a tragic incident late Friday night, Ishan Mehra, 32, Deputy Superintendent of Police at the National Investigation Agency (NIA), lost his life when his superbike collided with a container truck on...

item.title: US, UK, and Australia Develop Unmanned Undersea Vehicles to Safeguard Subsea Data Cables
item.description: The United States, the United Kingdom, and Australia are collaborating under their AUKUS security partnership to develop new unmanned undersea vehicles designed to protect critical subsea infrastructu...

item.title: CPKC maintains rail service amid strike by signalling and communications workers
item.description: Canadian Pacific Kansas City Ltd. (CPKC) is continuing rail operations across Canada despite a strike by nearly 300 signalling and communications workers represented by the International Brotherhood o...

item.title: Retired Army General and Wife Kidnapped in Katsina Amid Rising Banditry
item.description: Last Saturday, Major General Rabe Abubakar Batsari (rtd) and him wife, Hajiya Amina Abubakar, were abducted by armed bandits while dey dey travel through Matazu Local Government Area, Katsina State. T...

item.title: House fire in Scarborough prompts street closures and emergency response
item.description: A two-alarm house fire in Scarborough led to a significant emergency response and temporary street closures in the surrounding area on May 31, 2026. According to reports, Toronto Fire Services were ca...

item.title: Thiruvananthapuram Corporation introduces 'Mayor Care' initiative to support school students
item.description: The Thiruvananthapuram Corporation has launched a new civic initiative called ‘Mayor Care’, aimed at supporting school students from economically weaker backgrounds. The programme was inaugurated by M...

item.title: Kapamilya Stars JMFyang, KaiRen, and FranSeth Join Fans for Karaoke Segment on ASAP XP
item.description: Featured on ASAP XP, Kapamilya artists JMFyang, KaiRen, and FranSeth took part in a lively karaoke session together with fans, creating a fun and interactive entertainment segment. The activity highli...

item.title: Peter Murrell purchased 108 toilet rolls shortly before Covid panic-buying warnings issued during Scotland’s pandemic response
item.description: The article reports on claims relating to Peter Murrell, former chief executive of the Scottish National Party (SNP) and estranged husband of Nicola Sturgeon, Scotland’s former First Minister. Court d...

item.title: Kerala police arrest mother and live-in partner in toddler death case involving alleged prolonged abuse
item.description: A 1.5-year-old boy from Nedumangad in Kerala has died under extremely disturbing circumstances, with police arresting his mother Akhila (24) and her live-in partner Ashkar (31) on charges of murder an...

item.title: Soname rewards Nigeria U17 Flamingos with N4.5m after 11–0 aggregate win over Guinea
item.description: Nigeria U17 women’s national team, the Flamingos, don receive a cash reward of N4.5 million from Remo Stars and Beyond Limits Football Club owner, Hon. Kunle Soname, after their impressive performance...

item.title: Government plans to reduce toll exemptions on national highways, may remove some official categories
item.description: The central government is considering a revision of the existing rules governing toll exemptions on national highways, with a view to reducing the number of categories currently exempted. As per avail...
